label: CockroachDB
description: Connect to a CockroachDB cluster — serverless, dedicated, or self-hosted — using a Postgres-compatible SQL interface.

form.title: Connect CockroachDB
form.subtitle: Connect to your CockroachDB cluster using a connection string or individual credentials.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste the full PostgreSQL-compatible connection string from your CockroachDB Console or cluster config. Quickest path — one field, no manual assembly.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Connection String

CockroachDB clusters expose a single PostgreSQL-compatible connection string you can copy directly from the Console.

### CockroachDB Serverless / Dedicated (Cloud)

1. Sign in to [CockroachDB Cloud](https://cockroachlabs.cloud/).
2. Click on your cluster name.
3. Click **Connect** (top-right).
4. Choose **Connection string** from the **Select option** dropdown.
5. Select your **SQL user** and **database** from the dropdowns.
6. Click **Copy connection string** — it looks like:
   `postgresql://user:password@host:26257/defaultdb?sslmode=verify-full`
7. Paste it into the **Connection String** field below.

### Self-hosted CockroachDB

1. Your connection string follows the pattern:
   `postgresql://<user>:<password>@<host>:<port>/<database>?sslmode=require`
2. Default port is **26257**.
3. Adjust `sslmode` to match your cluster's TLS configuration (`require`, `verify-full`, or `disable` for local dev clusters).

### Notes

- The string contains your password — Anton stores it encrypted locally.
- For Serverless clusters, `sslmode=verify-full` and the `options=--cluster=<routing-id>` param may be required; copy the string from the Console to get these automatically.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full PostgreSQL-compatible connection string. Copy it directly from CockroachDB Cloud Console → Connect → Connection string.

methods.label: Host / Port / Credentials
methods.description: Enter your cluster host, port, database name, and credentials individually. Useful when you have individual connection parameters rather than a full connection string.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Host, Port, and Credentials

Use this method when you have individual connection parameters (host, port, user, password) rather than a single connection string.

### Finding your connection parameters

**CockroachDB Cloud:**
1. Sign in to [CockroachDB Cloud](https://cockroachlabs.cloud/) and open your cluster.
2. Click **Connect** → choose **Parameters only** (or extract values from the connection string).
3. Note the **Host**, **Port** (typically `26257`), **Database**, **User**, and **Password**.

**Self-hosted:**
1. The host is the address of any cluster node or your load balancer.
2. Default port is `26257`.
3. Use the SQL user you created with `CREATE USER` and set a password with `ALTER USER ... WITH PASSWORD`.

### SSL / TLS

- For production clusters, set **SSL Mode** to `verify-full` or at minimum `require`.
- For local development clusters started without TLS, use `disable`.
- Serverless and Dedicated Cloud clusters require `verify-full`.

### Notes

- Ensure the SQL user has `CONNECT` privilege on the target database.
- CockroachDB Serverless may require the cluster routing ID appended to the username (e.g. `user.routing-id`) — check the Cloud Console for the exact username format.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of your CockroachDB cluster or load balancer.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: Name of the database to connect to. CockroachDB's default database is `defaultdb`.

fields.name: username
fields.label: SQL Username
fields.description: SQL user with CONNECT privilege on the target database. For Serverless clusters, this may include a routing suffix (e.g. user.cluster-routing-id).

fields.name: ssl_mode
fields.label: SSL Mode
fields.description: TLS verification mode. Use verify-full for Cloud clusters, require for self-hosted with TLS, disable only for local development.
